executionValidation: Course 1 Linux code_input tasks must use fixedLanguage bash, recipeType shell_task, and mode terminal_workspace.
Use workspaceExpectations for requiredFiles, requiredFolders, forbiddenFiles, and entryFilePath.
Use terminalExpectations for requiredCommands, forbiddenCommands, outputContains, outputRegex, cwdContains, and cwdEndsWith.
Do not use checker.defaultRecipe workspace_expectations; Linux terminal grading is shell_task plus expectations.
Independent quiz/check exercises should use per-exercise terminal workspaces so starter files are not reused from a previous exercise.
Do not call Judge0 for Linux Terminal Fundamentals terminal tasks.
pedagogy: Keep tasks visible in the terminal and file explorer.
Do not ask learners to edit Bash scripts in Course 1 unless the topic explicitly introduces scripting.
Keep destructive commands constrained to the workspace and explain safe paths.
Require one standalone course-introduction sketch before the first technical sketch.
Reject generic help repeated across unrelated commands.
Require final capstones to use exactly one section, one topic, and multiple progressive steps.
Require the capstone to use inspection, navigation, creation, writing, viewing, copying, moving, renaming, safe deletion, and final verification.
